message:
feat(engine): add declassification receipt validation with pre-sign and pre-verify gating

Previously, DeclassificationReceipt.sign() and verify() operated on
whatever field values were present, including empty or whitespace-only
fields. A receipt with an empty receipt_id, declassification_route_ref,
decision_contract_id, policy_evaluation_summary, or replay_linkage
could be signed and verified, producing cryptographically valid but
semantically meaningless receipts that break deterministic replay and
decision-contract provenance chains.

Changes:
- Add DeclassificationReceipt::validate() that checks all five
  required linkage fields (receipt_id, declassification_route_ref,
  decision_contract_id, policy_evaluation_summary, replay_linkage)
  are non-empty after trimming, returning IfcValidationError on failure
- Gate sign() and verify() behind validate(), so a receipt with missing
  linkage fields is rejected before any cryptographic operation
- Add trimmed_artifact_id() and validate_required_field() helpers with
  structured error messages identifying the artifact kind, ID, and
  which field failed
- Add signature_error_from_validation() adapter to bridge
  IfcValidationError into SignatureError for the sign/verify paths
- Add integration tests covering empty-field rejection on both sign
  and verify paths

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
